entities.name: HARICOT BEAN
entities.description: An important legume crop (Phaseolus vulgaris L.) serving as a cash crop and protein source in Ethiopia

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the socio-agronomic study was conducted

entities.name: ADOPTION AND INTENSITY
entities.description: The extent and degree to which improved crop varieties and agronomic techniques are utilized by farmers

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Farming practices associated with the production of haricot beans

entities.name: PURPOSIVE SAMPLING
entities.description: A non-probability sampling technique used to choose rural kebeles

entities.name: SYSTEMATIC RANDOM SAMPLE
entities.description: A probability sampling method used to select technology user respondents

entities.name: FOCUS GROUP DISCUSSIONS
entities.description: A qualitative research method used to gather data from groups

entities.name: KEY INFORMANT INTERVIEWS
entities.description: A qualitative research method used to gather data from knowledgeable individuals

entities.name: TOBIT ECONOMETRIC MODEL
entities.description: A statistical model used to analyze the factors influencing the adoption and intensity of production practices

entities.name: GENDER OF THE HEAD OF THE HOUSEHOLD
entities.description: A socio-economic factor that positively influences the adoption of improved varieties

entities.name: IMPROVED TRAINING
entities.description: Educational programs in haricot bean production that positively influence adoption

entities.name: FIELD DAY PROGRAMS
entities.description: Extension activities that positively influence the adoption of improved varieties

entities.name: DEMONSTRATIONS
entities.description: Practical displays of improved techniques that positively influence adoption

entities.name: SEED CREDIT
entities.description: Financial access to improved seeds that positively influences adoption

entities.name: SEED MULTIPLICATION GROUP
entities.description: Membership in groups dedicated to seed production that positively influences adoption

entities.name: MARKET DISTANCE
entities.description: The physical distance to markets, which negatively influences adoption

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: A desired characteristic of haricot bean varieties based on farmer preference

entities.name: MARKET DEMAND
entities.description: An economic factor influencing the choice of improved varieties

entities.name: PRICE ADVANTAGE
entities.description: An economic benefit influencing the choice of improved varieties

entities.name: MATURITY TIME
entities.description: The duration from planting to harvest, a key selection criterion for farmers

entities.name: GRAIN COLOR
entities.description: A physical characteristic of the bean used by farmers for variety selection

entities.name: GRAIN SIZE
entities.description: A physical characteristic of the bean used by farmers for variety selection

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the crop to withstand diseases, a key selection criterion

entities.name: STORAGE PRIORITY
entities.description: The quality of the crop regarding its storage life

entities.name: NASIR CULTIVAR
entities.description: A specific improved variety of haricot bean ranked first by farmers

entities.name: DIMTU CULTIVAR
entities.description: A specific improved variety of haricot bean ranked second by farmers

entities.name: FERTILIZER
entities.description: A nutrient input whose application rate often differs from research recommendations

entities.name: EXTENSION ASSISTANCE
entities.description: Support from agricultural extension services that influences the adoption of improved cultivars

entities.name: MARKET INFORMATION
entities.description: Timely data on markets that is suggested to enhance the adoption of improved cultivars
